description: The deep wish for all beings to be free from suffering, coupled with the active commitment to help ease that suffering. It includes the concept of mercy as a natural expression of this state.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'lo' (connection) and 'thea' (a new proposed root for 'active, tender care'). The word means 'a connection of active care.' The fricative 'th' gives it a soft, heartfelt quality, while the vowel hiatus 'e.a' creates an open, offering sound.
